pi_items.source: Verified against the explicit task instructions, the export_result.sh script, and the verifier.py logic.
Verified as an accurate lower-bound threshold. A Subsurface-generated multi-page PDF for a 29-dive logbook will easily exceed 1KB in size.

Parsed the underlying SampleDivesV2.ssrf XML file directly. The strings 'Sund Rock', 'Yellow House', and 'Hoodsport' are confirmed present in the location fields. The string 'Washington' does not exist in the dataset (only the abbreviation 'WA, USA' is used), so it will not appear in the printed PDF.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires printing the real dive log dataset (SampleDivesV2.ssrf from the official Subsurface repo) to a PDF file at /home/ga/Documents/printed_log.pdf. Analysis of the source data confirms the presence of the text keywords 'Sund Rock', 'Yellow House', and 'Hoodsport'. However, the keyword 'Washington' is not present in the dataset (which instead uses the abbreviation 'WA'), meaning it will not appear in the resulting PDF. The file size threshold of 1024 bytes is a correct minimum bound, as the multi-page PDF will be much larger.
